revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jōurugami Kofun]][[en:Jōurugami Kofun]][[ja:條ウル神古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jōurugami Kofun]][[en:Jōurugami Kofun]][[ja:條ウル神古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jōurugami Kofun]][[en:Jōurugami Kofun]][[ja:條ウル神古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōurugami Kofun]][[ja:條ウル神古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Jōurugami Kofun | native_name =佐味田宝塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Jo Urugami Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jōurugami Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jōurugami Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.443125|N|135.744292|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|35.25|N|135|44|39.45|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Jōurugami Kofun'''|條ウル神古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the city of {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/544981|title=條ウル神古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Jōurugami Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. The {{ill|Koseyama Kofun Cluster|en|Koseyama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11479773|lt=Koseyama Kofun Cluster|1=Koseyama Kofun Cluster}} (more than 700 tombs in total) is distributed in the hills to the south, and this tumulus was designated Koseyama Kofun No.658 in 1984. The mound of this tumulus has been largely leveled, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been unable to confirm if the original configuration was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tumulus. The total length of the tumulus is about 70 meters and was orientated to the south-southeast. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior circular portion (northern mound), in which a house-shaped Sarcophagus is located. It is a large stone chamber more than 15.6 meters in total length. The size of the sarcophagus's cover stone is 278 cm in length, 147 cm in width, and 53 cm in height, making it comparable to the Gojyo-no-Maruyama Kofun (289 cm in length) from the same period. The cover stone also has eight rope protrusions, one on each side and three on each side, making it unusual compared to the two on each side of a typical house-shaped sarcophagus. The size of the burial chamber and sarcophagus is comparable to that of Gojyo-no-Maruyama Kofu in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}} (possibly the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}) and [[Ishibutai Kofun]] in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka, Nara}}, and the characteristics of the burial chamber and sarcophagus are the same as those of the surrounding Koseyama Kofun group. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines. The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the latter half of the 6th century during the late Kofun period.
It is also believed that another burial facility existed in the anterior portion (southern mound), as fragments of the stone walls of a burial chamber and stone material of the sarcophagus have been found, but the details are unclear as the mound has been was destroyed.
The tumulus is about 2.5 kilometers south of {{ill|Tamade Station (Nara)|en|Tamade Station (Nara)|qid=Q3611507|lt=Tamade Station|1=Tamade Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|qid=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000003961.html Gose city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00213.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
